meaning: n.猛拉,急推,蹒跚,故障vt.猛拉,系住,拴,钩住,套住vi.蹒跚,被钩住
examples: Do we need to be so wary both to hitch and to give a lift?
The ceremony went off without a hitch.
It might be longer than expected, but Zeller and Smith want Hitch to get as many rides as it can.
Easy, just hitch your wagon to an even brighter star as in Star Wars.
"But I predict the day will go off without a hitch, " says Mr Church.
